sources.full: Steinhardt's Guide to the Eldritch Hunt (2024)

entries: {@i Choose A, B, or C:} (A) {@item Handaxe|XPHB}, {@item Cleaver|SGttEH24}, {@item Blunderbuss|SGttEH24}, Pouch of 20 {@item Bullet|SGttEH24|Bullets}, {@item Scale Mail|XPHB}, {@item Explorer's Pack|XPHB}, and 1 GP; (B) {@item Rapier|XPHB}, {@item Scythe|SGttEH24}, {@item Pistol|SGttEH24}, Pouch of 20 {@item Bullet|SGttEH24|Bullets}, {@item Leather Armor|XPHB}, {@item Dungeoneer's Pack|XPHB}, and 29 GP; or (C) 115 GP

classFeature.name: Adrenaline
entries: Your focus during battle is razor sharp, letting you keenly use Adrenaline during critical moments. You have 1 Adrenaline Point. You gain additional Adrenaline Points at 2nd, 5th, 9th, 13th, and 17th level, as shown on the Jaeger table. You can expend Adrenaline Points to use {@filter Adrenaline Arts|optionalfeatures|feature type=sgtteh:aa}. You learn two such Adrenaline Arts: {@optfeature Weapon Parry|SGttEH24} and {@optfeature Dodge Step|SGttEH24}. You learn one additional Adrenaline Art of your choice at 2nd, 7th, 13th, and 17th level. Whenever you gain a Jaeger level, you can replace one of your Adrenaline Arts iwth another one. Your Adrenaline Art options are detailed at the end of the class description.

You regain all expended Adrenaline Points when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. When you roll a 20 on a saving throw or an attack roll against an enemy, you regain 1 Adrenaline Point. When you roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} and have no Adrenaline Points remaining, you regain 1 Adrenaline Point.

classFeature.name: Eldritch Hunter
entries: When you make an ability check to track or identify an Aberration, Celestial, Fiend, Monstrosity, or Undead, you can add your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} to the ability check. If you are already proficient in the ability check, you can double your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}.

classFeature.name: Flexible Combatant
entries: You can draw or stow two one-handed weapons when you would normally be able to draw or stow only one, and you can reload weapons with the {@itemProperty LD|XPHB|Loading} or {@itemProperty SGttEH:Bar|SGttEH24|Barrel} properties without a free hand.

Additionally, if you are wielding a weapon in one hand, and a one-handed ranged weapon in the other, you do not have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} from being within 5 feet of an enemy on attacks made with that ranged weapon.

classFeature.name: Weapon Mastery
entries: Your training with weapons allows you to use the mastery properties of two kinds of Simple or Martial weapons of your choice. Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can practice weapon drills and change one of those weapon choices.

classFeature.name: Fighting Style
entries: You gain a {@filter Fighting Style feat|feats|category=FS} of your choice. Instead of choosing one of those feats, you can choose one of the options below.

classFeature.name: Momentum
entries: In combat, you can build up Momentum, a force that empowers your strikes and enables brutal finishing moves.

entries.name: Momentum Dice
entries: Each time you expend an Adrenaline Point, you gain 1 Momentum die. Your Momentum die is a {@dice d6}, and it increases as you gain Jaeger levels, as shown in the Jaeger Features table. You can hold a maximum number of Momentum dice equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} + your Strength or Dexterity modifier (whichever is higher).
While you have 1 or more Momentum dice, your Momentum follows the rules below.

entries.name: Finishers
entries: You can expend all of your Momentum dice to execute a {@filter Finisher|optionalfeatures|feature type=sgtteh:f}. You know the {@optfeature Brutal|SGttEH24} Finisher. You learn one additional Finisher of your choice when you reach Jaeger levels 4, 6, 8, and 12, and may gain others through Jaeger Chapter features. Whenever you gain a Jaeger level, you can replace one of your Finishers with another one.

entries.name: Duration
entries: Your Momentum dice last until the end of your next turn. If you still have Momentum dice at the start of your next turn, you can keep them for another round by doing one of the following:

items: Gain a Momentum die.
Make an attack roll against a hostile creature.
End your turn within 5 feet of a hostile creature.

entries: Each time your Momentum dice are sustained this way, they last until the end of your next turn.

classFeature.name: Jaeger Subclass
entries: You gain a Jaeger subclass of your choice. A subclass is a specialization that grants you features at certain Jaeger levels. For the rest of your career, you gain each of your subclass's features that are of your Jaeger level or lower.

classFeature.name: Piercing Gaze
entries: You gain the ability to activate a magical sight at will (no action required), allowing you to effortlessly pierce the gloom and see what lurks within. For 1 hour, you gain {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} out to a range of 60 feet. If you already have {@sense Darkvision|XPHB}, its range increases to 120 feet. This vision lets you see normally in {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} and {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB}—both magical and nonmagical (such as the magical {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB} caused by the {@spell Darkness|XPHB} spell).

When you reach 7th level the {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} granted by this feature becomes permanent, but when activating the feature you gain the effect of {@spell See Invisibility|XPHB} for the duration. When you reach 13th level, you additionally gain the effect of {@spell True Seeing|XPHB} for the duration when activating the feature.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you complete a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: You gain the {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB} feat or another {@5etools feat|feats.html} of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Jaegar levels 8, 10, 12, and 16.

classFeature.name: Seasoned Survivor
entries: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} checks made to find secret passages, interpret markings or messages left by other creatures on walls or surfaces, or determine the fate of creatures from blood stains and remains.

classFeature.name: Extra Attack
entries: You can attack twice instead of once whenever you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn.

classFeature.name: Hunter's Pursuit
entries: At the start of your turn, you can expend 1 Adrenaline Point to immediately move up to half your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} without using any of your movement and without provoking {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}.

classFeature.name: Subclass Feature
entries: You gain a feature from your Jaeger Subclass.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: You gain the {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB} feat or another {@5etools feat|feats.html} of your choice for which you qualify.

classFeature.name: Evasion
entries: When you're subjected to an effect that allows you to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage if you succeed on the saving throw and only half damage if you fail. You can't use this feature if you have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: You gain the {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB} feat or another {@5etools feat|feats.html} of your choice for which you qualify.

classFeature.name: Lethal Tempo
entries: The first time you hit a creature on your turn, you gain 1 Momentum die. You gain 1 additional Momentum die any time you reduce a creature to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: You gain the {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB} feat or another {@5etools feat|feats.html} of your choice for which you qualify.

classFeature.name: Relentless Pursuit
entries: When you use your {@classFeature Hunter's Pursuit|Jaeger|SGttEH24|6}, if you end your movement next to a hostile creature, you regain the expended Adrenaline Point.

classFeature.name: Subclass Feature
entries: You gain a feature from your Jaeger Subclass.

classFeature.name: Inured to Madness
entries: You gain {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} and {@condition Frightened|XPHB} conditions, and against effects that cause Madness. If you fail a saving throw against any of these, you can expend 1 Adrenaline Point to reroll the die. You must use the new roll.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: You gain the {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB} feat or another {@5etools feat|feats.html} of your choice for which you qualify.

classFeature.name: Subclass Feature
entries: You gain a feature from your Jaeger Subclass.

classFeature.name: Eternal Watch
entries: You are always under the effect of your {@classFeature Piercing Gaze|Jaeger|SGttEH24|3}.

classFeature.name: Epic Boon
entries: You gain an {@filter Epic Boon feat|feats|category=EB} or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. {@feat Boon of Combat Prowess|XPHB} is recommended.

classFeature.name: Always Ready
entries: Once per round (beginning at the start of each of your turns), you gain one additional {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}, which you can only use on an Adrenaline Art that requires a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} (such as {@optfeature Weapon Parry|SGttEH24} or {@optfeature Dodge Step|SGttEH24}). When you expend an Adrenaline Point on this special {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}, you immediately regain the expended Adrenaline Point.

otherSummary.entry: When Gaining the Level 2 Jaegar "Fighting Style" Feature

items: When you attack with a weapon you are wielding in one hand, you can make an extra attack as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} with a different weapon you are wielding in one hand, and you don't add your ability modifier to the extra attack's damage unless that modifier is negative. You can't benefit from this feature if you've used the {@itemMastery Nick|XPHB} Weapon Mastery this turn.
While wielding two weapons, you gain +1 to the damage rolls of both weapons.

feat.name: Focused Fighting

otherSummary.entry: When Gaining the Level 2 Jaegar "Fighting Style" Feature

entries: You learn an additional {@filter Adrenaline Art|optionalfeatures|feature type=sgtteh:aa}, and you gain 1 additional Adrenaline Point.

feat.name: Curious
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Learn
entries: Whenever you make an ability check using a skill you are not proficient in, you can become proficient in that skill until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. Once you use this benefit, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Observe
entries: Immediately after you roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB}, you can choose one creature you can see in the same combat. You learn any of the creature's Resistances and Vulnerabilities.

feat.name: Faithful
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Divine Clarity
entries: If you fail a saving throw against the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} or {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition, you can choose to succeed instead. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Trial of Faith
entries: Whenever you roll a 1 on a {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB}, you gain {@variantrule Heroic Inspiration|XPHB}.

feat.name: Grizzled
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Overcome
entries: Whenever you make a saving throw against the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition, you gain a bonus to the saving throw equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}.

entries.name: Survive
entries: You require half as much food and water to survive, and you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Survival|XPHB}) checks made to forage for food and water.

entries.name: Withstand
entries: Whenever you become {@status Bloodied|XPHB}, you gain {@variantrule Heroic Inspiration|XPHB}.

feat.name: Worthless
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Worthless
entries: When you gain this Origin Feat, reduce your highest ability score (your choice if they are equal) until the sum of your six ability scores is equal to or lower than 72. Then, reduce each ability score by 1.

entries.name: From the Ashes
entries: When you reach character level 4, and again at character levels 8, 12, 16, and 19, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. You can't increase an ability score above 18 using this benefit.

feat.name: Brutalizer
entries: You master a reckless approach that maximizes your offensive capabilities. You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Brutal Weaponry
entries: You can wield a weapon with the {@itemProperty 2H|XPHB|Two-Handed} property in one hand, as long as your other hand is empty or wielding a one-handed weapon.

entries.name: Killing Streak
entries: When you reduce a creature to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, your next attack roll before the end of your next turn has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}.

feat.name: Cannoneer
entries: The best defense is a good offense, and the best offense is a cannon; you gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Cannon Proficiency
entries: You gain proficiency with Cannons, and you can reload a Cannon as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} instead of an action. You can take this {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} only if you haven't moved during your turn, and after you take the {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is 0 until the end of your turn. Starting at character level 11, this doesn't reduce your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}, and when you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action, you can replace one of your attacks with reloading the Cannon. When you reach character level 20, you can ignore the Artillery property of Cannons.

entries.name: Siege
entries: Your attacks with Cannons deal double damage to objects and structures.

entries.name: Strong Back
entries: The weight of Cannons and Cannonballs count as half for you.

feat.name: Cosmos Touched
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Cosmos Magic
entries: Choose one level 1 spell from the Abjuration or Transmutation school of magic. You always have that spell and the {@spell Alter Self|XPHB} spell prepared. You can cast each of these spells without a spell slot. Once you cast either spell in this way, you can't cast that spell in this way again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also cast these spells using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level. The spells' spellcasting ability is the ability increased by this feat.

feat.name: Focused Hunter
entries: During hunts, you are capable of bursts of focus when you need them most. You gain the following benefits:

items.name: Improved Focus
entries: You gain 1 Adrenaline Point (adding to your existing Adrenaline Points if you already have Adrenaline Points), and learn one new Adrenaline Art, choosing from {@optfeature Weapon Parry|SGttEH24}, {@optfeature Dodge Step|SGttEH24}, or any of the Jaeger's Additional {@filter Adrenaline Arts|optionalfeatures|feature type=sgtteh:aa}. You regain all expended Adrenaline Points when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

feat.name: Marksman
entries: You know your firearms inside and out and are a master at handling them. You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Fast Hands
entries: You can reload weapons with the {@itemProperty SGttEH:Bar|SGttEH24|Barrel} property as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} even if they do not possess the {@itemProperty L|XPHB|Light} property. If a weapon possesses both the {@itemProperty SGttEH:Bar|SGttEH24|Barrel} and {@itemProperty L|XPHB|Light} properties, you can ignore the {@itemProperty SGttEH:Bar|SGttEH24|Barrel} property.

entries.name: Steady Aim
entries: Being within 5 feet of an enemy doesn't impose {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on your ranged attack rolls, nor do you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on ranged attacks against creatures with the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition within 30 feet of you.

feat.name: Osteomantic Adept
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Empowered Osteomancy
entries: When you cast an {@filter Osteomancy spell of level 1 or higher|spells|level=!0|subschool=osteomancy}, you can sacrifice your life force to empower it, increasing the level this spell is cast at by one. You then roll a number of d6s equal to half the original spell's level (rounded up, minimum of 1) and take Necrotic damage equal to the number rolled, which can't be reduced or prevented in any way.
The first time you use this benefit after you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, it doesn't damage you.

items.name: Equipment:

items.name: Tool Proficiency:
items.entry: Choose one kind of {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB} related to your investigative methods

items.name: Equipment:
items.entry: Choose A or B: (A) {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB} (same as above), {@item Paper|XPHB} (10 sheets), {@item Traveler's Clothes|XPHB}, 32 GP; or (B) 50 GP

items.entry: {@item Tinker's Tools|XPHB}; Choose one kind of {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB}

items.name: Equipment:
items.entry: Choose A or B: (A) {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB} (same as above), {@item Ink|XPHB}, {@item Ink Pen|XPHB}, {@item Paper|XPHB} (50 sheets), {@item Tinker's Tools|XPHB}, {@item Traveler's Clothes|XPHB}, 3 GP; or (B) 50 GP

items.name: Equipment:
items.entry: Choose A or B: (A) {@item Dagger|XPHB}, sewer grate key, {@item Traveler's Clothes|XPHB}, 5 GP; or (B) 10 GP

spell.name: Bestial Roar
entries: You release a loud, monstrous roar mimicking those of ravenous beasts, causing shockwaves around you. Each creature in a 10-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from you is pushed 10 feet away from you and must succeed on a Strength saving throw or have the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Cantrip Upgrade
entries: The size of the {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} increases by 5 feet when you reach levels 5 (15 feet), 11 (20 feet), and 17 (25 feet).

spell.name: Brittle Bone Throw
entries: You hurl a splintered bone fragment at a creature within range. Make a ranged spell attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 1d8} Piercing damage. On a miss, you can shatter the bone mid-air and redirect the remnant toward another creature within 30 feet of the original target. Make another ranged spell attack. On a hit, the new target takes {@damage 1d4} Piercing damage.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Cantrip Upgrade
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d8} and {@dice 1d4}, respectively, when you reach levels 5 ({@dice 2d8}; {@dice 2d4}), 11 ({@dice 3d8}; {@dice 3d4}), and 17 ({@dice 4d8}; {@dice 4d4}).

spell.name: Cosmic Eye
entries: Your eye darkens under the influence of the eldritch madness, appearing as an endless starry sky. Make a ranged spell attack against a creature within range as you fire a projectile of eldritch energy from your eye. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 1d8} Necrotic damage. If you score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB}, you can treat any roll of 4 or lower on the damage die as a 5.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Cantrip Upgrade
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d8} when you reach levels 5 ({@dice 2d8}), 11 ({@dice 3d8}), and 17 ({@dice 4d8}).

spell.name: Gravity Spike
entries: You create a localized gravitational field that temporarily shifts the landscape to impale a creature. Choose a creature on the ground you can see within range. The creature must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, the target takes {@damage 1d6} Piercing damage and has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage only.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Cantrip Upgrade
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d6} when you reach levels 5 ({@dice 2d6}), 11 ({@dice 3d6}), and 17 ({@dice 4d6}).

spell.name: Gravity Whip
entries: You weave gravity in your palm before striking. Make a melee spell attack against a creature within 10 feet of you. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 1d4} Bludgeoning damage and is pushed 5 feet in a straight line in a direction of your choice. In addition, its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced by 10 feet until the start of your next turn.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Cantrip Upgrade
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d4} and the distance a creature is pushed increases by 5 feet when you reach levels 5 ({@dice 2d4}; 10 feet), 11 ({@dice 3d4}; 15 feet), and 17 ({@dice 4d4}; 20 feet).

spell.name: Bone Claws
entries: Choose a creature you can see within range. You infuse them with osteomantic energy, growing sharp claws from their carpal bones. For the duration, the target's {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB|Unarmed Strikes} deal an extra {@damage 1d4} Slashing damage on a hit.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases with a spell slot of level 2 ({@dice 1d6}), 3 ({@dice 2d4}), 4 ({@dice 2d6}), and 5+ ({@dice 3d4}).

spell.name: Bone Shield

time.condition: which you take when you are hit by an attack

entries: You generate a barrier of elongated femurs that protects you. You reduce the incoming damage by {@dice 1d12}, and if the attacker is within 10 feet of you, the osseous barrier explodes, dealing Slashing damage to the attacker equal to the amount reduced.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d12} for each spell slot level above 1.

spell.name: Electrify
entries: You channel lightning into your hands. The next time you hit a creature with a melee attack roll (including a melee spell attack) before the start of your next turn, the target takes {@damage 1d12} Lightning damage and makes a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the target has the {@condition Stunned|XPHB} condition until the start of its next turn.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d12} for each spell slot level above 1.

spell.name: Fall
entries: You can temporarily change which way gravity pulls you and immediately fall 200 feet in that direction. If you hit a solid surface, you take damage from the fall based on the distance you traveled.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The distance you fall increases by 50 feet for each spell slot level above 1.

spell.name: Fractured Shell
entries: You touch a willing creature, forcing spiked bone plates to grow and pierce through its skin. It takes 1 Piercing damage. For the duration of the spell, the first time each turn that the target is hit with a melee attack roll, the attacking creature takes {@damage 2d4} Piercing damage.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage the attacker takes increases by {@dice 2d4} for each spell slot level above 1. If you use a level 5+ spell slot, the spell doesn't require {@status Concentration|XPHB}, and the duration becomes 10 minutes.

spell.name: Gravity Leap
entries: You enhance the gravitational field around your body, allowing you to jump higher and farther. For the duration, your jump distance is tripled. Additionally, once per turn immediately after you jump, a wave of gravity is unleashed, and each creature within 5 feet of the space you left must make a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, a target has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.

spell.name: Gravity Well
entries: You fire a projectile of condensed gravitational force toward a creature you can see within range. Make a ranged spell attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 2d8} Force damage and is pulled 20 feet in a straight line toward you.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d8} for each spell slot level above 1.

spell.name: Phalangeal Shot
entries: You point your fingers in a direction of your choice, and your phalangeal bones fire in a 30-foot-long, 1-foot-wide {@variantrule Line [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Line}, akin to the bullets of a gun. They regrow immediately, leaving your hand unscathed. Each creature in the {@variantrule Line [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Line} must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take {@damage 2d8} Piercing damage.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d8} for each spell slot level above 1.

spell.name: Silvered Shell
entries: You touch a weapon and imbue it with otherworldly bile. For the duration, it becomes Moongilded, and while you are holding it, it deals an extra {@damage 1d4} Force damage on a hit.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The duration increases with a spell slot of level 2 (10 minutes), 3 (1 hour), 4 (8 hours), or 5+ (24 hours).

spell.name: Spectral Slash

m.text: a melee weapon worth at least 1 SP+

entries: You send forth a spectral copy of yourself to strike down your foe. Make a melee spell attack against a creature within 20 feet of you. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 1d8} damage of your weapon's damage type.

You can then take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to move up to 20 feet in a straight line toward the target, streaking through a spectral trail, and take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action against it with the weapon used in the spell's casting.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d8} and the distance up to which you can move increases by 10 feet for each spell slot level above 1.

spell.name: Arm Cannon
entries: You fire out the bones of your forearm through your hand, leaving a severe wound that immediately regrows and heals. Make a ranged spell attack against a creature within range. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 4d8} Piercing damage and must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be pushed 15 feet away from you. If pushed into an obstacle, the target is impaled by the bone and has the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition until the end of its next turn. Creatures that are Large or larger have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on this saving throw.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d8} and the distance a creature is pushed increases by 5 feet for each spell slot level above 2.

spell.name: Blind Ambush
entries: You blow on a whistle, releasing an inaudible sound. Choose a point on the ground within range. The first time a creature moves 5 feet or more while within a 10- foot square centered on that point before the spell ends, an eldritch maw immediately erupts from beneath the ground, dealing {@damage 2d10} Piercing damage to each creature within the square and knocking one of the creatures {@condition Prone|XPHB} (decide when the spell triggers). The spell then ends.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d10} for each spell slot level above 2. If you use a level 5+ spell slot, the spell can trigger a second time before it ends.

spell.name: Bludgeoning Horror

components.m: the body part of a Great One

entries: You summon a large number of tentacles and slugs from your palm to strike a creature within range. Make a melee spell attack against the target. On a hit, it takes {@damage 3d6} Bludgeoning damage. If you score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB}, the target also has the {@condition Stunned|XPHB} condition until the start of your next turn.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d6} and the range increases by 5 feet for each spell slot level above 2.

spell.name: Bone Cocoon

time.condition: which you take when you see a creature drop to 0 hit points

m.text: a shrunk and gilded rib cage worth at least 50+ GP

entries: You conjure a cocoon made of cartilage and bones, which protects the creature that dropped to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. While in the cocoon, the creature can't be damaged by attacks or effects originating from outside it, has {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Total Cover}, has the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition, and makes {@variantrule Death Saving Throw|XPHB|Death Saving Throws} with {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}.

The cocoon has 25 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} and an AC of 11 plus your spellcasting ability modifier. It has {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Cold, Fire, and Necrotic damage and {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to Poison and Psychic damage. If you lose {@status Concentration|XPHB} or the cocoon is reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, it is destroyed and the spell ends.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The cocoon's {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} increase by 25 for each spell slot level above 2.

spell.name: Calcified Memories
entries: You touch the bones of a deceased creature. When you do so, a ghostly gray illusion of the fallen creature appears above its corpse to reenact the last 6 seconds of its life. The illusion only shows the creature's actions, such as reacting to an unseen monster or choking from poisonous gas, but does not show the monster or effect causing the behavior, only their outline. This spell has no effect on Undead creatures. Once a corpse's death has been revealed in this way, it can't be shown again for 24 hours.

spell.name: Graviturgic Smite
entries: The next time you hit a creature with a weapon attack before this spell ends, you can add energy stolen from black holes. Your attack deals an extra {@damage 3d4} Bludgeoning damage to the target, which must make a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, the target's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced to 10 feet until the spell ends. On a successful save, its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced by 10 feet until the spell ends, to a minimum of 10 feet.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 2d4} for each spell slot level above 2.

spell.name: Gravity Storm
entries: You create a localized gravitational field that unleashes a storm of gravity waves. Choose a point within range that you can see. When you cast this spell and at the start of each of your subsequent turns for the duration, each creature in a 20-foot {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} originating from that point makes a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes {@damage 2d6} Bludgeoning damage and has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition. On a successful save, a target takes half as much damage only.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 2d6}, the {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere}'s radius increases by 5 feet, and the duration increases by 1 round for each spell slot level above 2.

spell.name: Lightning Charged

components.m: a piece of metal once used in a lightning rod

entries: You channel lightning energy into a creature. The energy is harmless to the creature but escapes in dangerous bursts to other nearby creatures.

Each time that creature affects another creature with a spell that has a range of Touch, strikes another creature with a melee attack roll, or ends its turn while grappling or being {@condition Grappled|XPHB} by another creature, the other creature takes {@damage 1d6} Lightning damage.
Once this spell has discharged 6 times (dealing up to {@damage 6d6} Lightning damage), the spell ends.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The spell can discharge damage 2 additional times (dealing up to {@dice 2d6} more total damage) before the spell ends for each spell slot level above 2.

spell.name: Otherworldly Gaze
entries: You tap into the eldritch forces of the universe, gaining heightened senses and the ability to see beyond the veil of reality. For the duration, your eyes turn black and you gain the following benefits:

items: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Perception|XPHB}) checks.
You sense the presence of eldritch creatures within 120 feet of you, including Aberrations, Celestials, and Fiends.
You can see the true form of any shapechanger or creature that is transformed by magic within 30 feet of you.
If you have the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} condition, you ignore the effects of the condition for the duration of this spell.

spell.name: Pressure Cage

components.m: a shard of ball and chain

entries: Gravity increases in a 10-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} centered on a point you choose within range. The area becomes {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB} as immense pressure is applied to all within. When a creature enters the {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there, it must succeed on a Strength saving throw or its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced to 0 until the start of its next turn.
On subsequent turns, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to move the sphere up to 20 feet in any direction.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere}'s radius increases by 5 feet for each spell slot level above 2.

spell.name: Rolling Bones

m.text: the bones of a small mammal worth at least 1 GP+

entries: You roll bones, allowing fate to give you an omen of the future. Roll {@dice 1d6}.

items: On a roll of 5 or 6, you know if you are: not likely, likely, or very likely to encounter {@variantrule Hostile [Attitude]|XPHB|Hostile} creatures within the next hour. You also learn the creature type you're most likely to encounter.
On a roll of 2, 3, or 4, you learn if you are: not likely, likely, or very likely to encounter {@variantrule Hostile [Attitude]|XPHB|Hostile} creatures within the next hour.

On a roll of 1, the Material components of the spell are consumed, and you take 1 Psychic damage as fate rebels. You don't learn any information and you can't cast this spell again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}.

entries: If you cast the spell two or more times before finishing a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, there is a cumulative {@chance 25} chance for each casting after the first that you get a false reading. The GM makes this roll in secret.

spell.name: Rupturing Curse
entries: You focus your magic to shatter the body of your foe. Choose one creature you can see within range; it must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or take {@damage 3d10} Piercing damage and have the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.

In addition, if the target takes Bludgeoning damage equal to 5 times its Constitution modifier (minimum of 5) or more in a single turn, its skeleton shatters, reducing its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} to 0 for the duration of the spell. If it does not take such damage before the start of its next turn, the spell ends. A creature without bones is immune to this effect.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d10} for each spell slot level above 2.

spell.name: Skeletal Tail
entries: You grow a long, scorpion-like tail made of sharpened bones. Whenever a creature moves into or within a 15-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from you, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to try and pierce the creature with your tail. Make a melee spell attack against the creature. On a hit, the target takes Piercing damage equal to {@dice 2d4} plus your spellcasting modifier and is pulled to the nearest unoccupied space to you as your tail drags it in.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 2d4} for each spell slot level above 2.

spell.name: Astral Barrage
entries: You create a portal to the cosmos and send asteroids hurtling through it at a creature you can see within range. The target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take {@damage 7d6} Bludgeoning damage. You can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action on subsequent turns to keep the portal open and send asteroids hurtling through it again. The spell ends if you use your action to do anything else. Your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is halved until this spell ends

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 2d6} for each spell slot level above 3.

spell.name: Dampen Gravity
entries: You focus your powers to lessen the call of gravity in a 60-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} around you. Each creature of your choice in the {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} has its jump distance doubled and its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increased by 10 feet. An affected creature also has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics|XPHB}) checks and ignores damage from falling if it ends its fall in the {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation}.

spell.name: Displacing Maw
entries: Your rib cage opens up violently, forming a maw that attempts to devour a creature within range. The creature must make a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, the target takes {@damage 4d8} Piercing damage and is transported through magic to a point of your choice on the ground within 60 feet of you, where the maw reappears and regurgitates them. On a successful save, it takes half as much damage only.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d8} and the maximum teleportation distance increases by 10 feet for each spell slot level above 3.

spell.name: Malicious Rancor

m.text: a skull that belonged to a creature that succumbed to a curse, worth at least 1 SP+, which the spell consumes

entries: You hurl a deformed skull covered in scratches and holes and strewn in curses at a creature within range, where the skull explodes with violent hatred. Make a ranged spell attack against the creature. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 1d10} Necrotic damage. Hit or miss, the skull then detonates, emitting a psychic wailing from that point. The target and each creature in a 10-foot- radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} centered on the target must make a Wisdom saving throw, taking {@damage 5d4} Psychic damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. Until the start of your next turn, whenever a creature enters the {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} or ends its turn there, it must make the same saving throw. A creature makes this save only once per turn.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The Psychic damage increases by {@dice 2d4} for each spell slot level above 3.

spell.name: Osseous Cage
entries: You cause cartilage and bones to erupt from the ground, forming a cage surrounding a 10-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} centered on you. The bones are thick and provide Three- Quarters {@variantrule Cover|XPHB} for creatures inside and outside. Creatures, including yourself, can't pass through the cage unless you take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} when a creature attempts to cross to let it through. A Large or larger can't enter the cage and is pushed to the nearest unoccupied space if it is within the cage's area when it first appears.

The osseous cage has AC 12, 100 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, and {@variantrule Vulnerability|XPHB} to Bludgeoning damage. If you cast this spell again while you already have a cage summoned, the previous cage turns to dust.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The cage has an additional 20 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} for each slot level above 3.

spell.name: Osseous Impalement
entries: Massive bone spikes sprout under up to four creatures on the ground of your choice that you can see within range. Each creature must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take {@damage 3d10} Piercing damage and be impaled by a spike for the duration. An impaled creature is held aloft 5 feet in the air, has the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition, and takes {@damage 1d10} Piercing damage at the start of each of its turns.
An impaled creature can take an action to make a Strength ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) check against your spell save DC, breaking the bone spike and ending the effect on itself on a success.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage (both initial and ongoing) increases by {@dice 1d10} for each spell slot level above 3.

spell.name: Radiant Bell

m.text: a silvered choir bell* worth at least 5 GP+, which the spell consumes

entries: You ring an arcane, silver bell, imbuing it with healing magics that cure the afflicted. Choose any number of creatures within 30 feet of you. For each target, you can end one of the following conditions: {@condition Charmed|XPHB}, {@condition Frightened|XPHB}, {@condition Poisoned|XPHB}, {@condition Petrified|XPHB}, or {@condition Stunned|XPHB}. This cure does not work for a target if it has the {@condition Deafened|XPHB} condition.

spell.name: Radiant Slaughter

components.m: the petrified fetus of a Great One

entries: You summon a small nova of eldritch power in this secret technique from the Obitus Scholare. Each creature in a 30-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from you must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes {@damage 2d6} Force damage plus {@damage 3d6} Radiant damage and has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition. On a successful save, it takes half as much damage only. Creatures within 5 feet of you make this saving throw with {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB}.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The Force and Radiant damage increase by {@dice 1d6} for each spell slot level above 3.

spell.name: Rubber Bones
entries: You touch a willing creature. Its bones become soft and rubbery, and it gains the following benefits:

items: It can spend 5 feet of movement to end the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition on itself without requiring an ability check.
It can squeeze through a space that is sized for a creature two sizes smaller than it.
It has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Dexterity saving throws.
It has {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Bludgeoning damage.
Its reach with melee attacks increases by 5 feet. Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You can target one additional creature for each spell slot level above 3.

spell.name: Spectral Fury

m.text: a melee weapon worth at least 1 SP+

entries: You send forth three spectral duplicates of yourself to strike down enemies within 30 feet of you. You can order them to strike one target or several. Make a melee spell attack for each spectral duplicate. On a hit, a target takes {@damage 4d8} damage of the type of the weapon used in the spell's casting.

You can then take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to move up to 30 feet in a straight line toward one of the targets without provoking {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}, streaking through a spectral trail, and make one melee attack with a weapon.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage of your spectral duplicate increases by {@dice 2d8} for each spell slot level above 3.

spell.name: Dread Scarecrow

m.text: a flute made of a femur worth 50 GP+

entries: You magically create the horrifying illusion of all your bones breaking and your body bending in impossible ways, accompanied by the sound of a shattered skull. Each creature of your choice in a 30-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from you must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or have the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition for the duration. While {@condition Frightened|XPHB} by this spell, a creature's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is 0, and it has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition, its legs giving out under fear. If an affected creature takes damage, it repeats the save at the end of its next turn, ending the effect on itself on a success.

spell.name: Fling
entries: You flick your finger against a creature. The target is impacted by a devastating gravitational force and must make a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, the target takes {@damage 6d10} Bludgeoning damage and is pushed 120 feet away from you. The push stops early if the creature hits a solid surface or is pushed into a creature of its size or larger. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage and isn't pushed.

If the target is pushed into or through the space of a creature, that creature makes a Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 1d10} Bludgeoning damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. This damage increases by {@dice 1d10} per size smaller the creature is than the target.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage to the target increases by {@dice 1d10} and the pushing distance increases by 30 feet for each spell slot level above 4.

Note: For example, if a {@creature Commoner|XMM} (size Medium) is pushed into a {@creature Frog|XMM} (size Tiny), the frog must make a Dexterity saving throw. Because the frog is 2 sizes smaller than the commoner, on a failed save, it would take {@damage 3d10} damage (the original {@damage 1d10} damage plus the {@dice 2d10} increase), exploding on impact, whilst the commoner  would  continue  its  destructive  flight unhindered—questioning the laws of physics.

spell.name: Gravitational Distortion
entries: Reality swirls in a 30-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} centered on a point you can see within range. Each creature in the area makes a Strength saving throw, taking {@damage 6d6} Bludgeoning damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. Additionally, any creature that fails this saving throw is pushed 30 feet in a straight line in a direction of your choice.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d6} for each spell slot level above 4.

spell.name: Gravity Barrier
entries: You create a wall of increased gravity on the ground at a point you can see within range. You can make a straight wall up to 30 feet long, 10 feet high, and 1 foot thick, or a ringed wall up to 20 feet in diameter, 20 feet high, and 1 foot thick. The wall lasts for the duration and its space is {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}.

Ranged weapon attacks that pass through the wall's space automatically fail, and other ranged attack rolls have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB}. Any creature that passes through the wall's space must succeed on a Strength saving throw or take {@damage 4d8} Bludgeoning damage, have the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition, and have its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} reduced to 0 until the end of its turn.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d8} for each spell slot level above 4.

spell.name: Jumping Jolt
entries: You release an arc of lightning at a creature within range. Make a ranged spell attack against the creature. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 4d12} Lightning damage, and you can cause the spell to jump to another creature within 20 feet of the target. On a miss, the target takes half as much damage only. When the spell jumps, make another ranged spell attack against the new target, with the same effect on a hit or miss, respectively. The spell can't hit the same target twice or jump to a target out of the spell's range. The spell can jump to a new target up to four times.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d12} and the number of times the spell can jump increases by one for each spell slot level above 4.

spell.name: Maiden of Bones
entries: A creature within range must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be trapped inside an iron maiden formed of bone that appears in its space. The target has the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} and {@condition Restrained|XPHB} conditions, is behind {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Total Cover}, can't be damaged by attacks or effects originating from outside the maiden, and takes {@damage 3d6} Piercing damage at the start of each of its turns as spikes of bone pierce its body.
At the end of each of its turns, a trapped creature repeats the save, escaping and ending the spell on a success.

The bone maiden has an AC equal to 10 plus your spellcasting modifier, 80 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to Psychic and Poison damage, and {@variantrule Vulnerability|XPHB} to Bludgeoning damage. If the maiden is destroyed, the creature trapped inside is freed and the spell ends.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d6} and the maiden's {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} increase by 20 for each spell slot level above 4.

spell.name: Marrow Transplant

m.text: a piece of spinal bone and a diamond worth at least 300 GP+, which the spell consumes

entries: You drain the bone marrow of a creature, attempting to steal its life force and transfer it to another creature of your choice. Make a ranged spell attack against a creature within range. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 4d6} Necrotic damage. Choose another creature within range; it regains a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the damage dealt, or if it was dead for less than 1 minute, the creature returns to life with {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the number regained. If any of the targets do not have bones, the spell fails. This spell can't return to life a creature that has died of old age, nor can it restore any missing body parts.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d6} for each spell slot level above 4.

spell.name: Vanishing Step

components.m: the bone of an old hunter

entries: For the duration, once on each of your turns, you can use 15 feet of your movement to teleport to an unoccupied space you can see within 15 feet of yourself. You can't teleport in this way if you have the {@condition Prone|XPHB} or {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition.

In addition, when you are hit by an attack or targeted by a spell, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to teleport to an unoccupied space you can see within 15 feet of you, causing the attack to miss you or the spell not to affect you if you leave its range or radius. When you do so, until the end of your next turn, your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced by 15 feet.

spell.name: World Breaker
entries: You destroy your surroundings to break your foes. You rip a 5-foot by 5-foot chunk of nonmagical terrain from the ground and hurl it at a point you can see within range, where it shatters in a 30-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone} extending away from you.
Each creature in the area must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take {@damage 4d10} Bludgeoning damage and be pushed 15 feet away from you.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d10} for each spell slot level above 4.

spell.name: Ancestral Bond

m.text: the corpse of a humanoid, which the spell may consume

entries: You touch the corpse of a Humanoid. Ephemeral spirits scour the world, and visions of the Humanoid's descendants come to you. You learn how many living relatives it left behind and if it has any living descendants.

In addition, you can select one of the descendants and destroy the corpse to amplify the power of the magic. The descendant makes a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, you learn the target's exact location, no matter where it is.

spell.name: Chisel Skull
entries: Choose a creature you can see within range. You cause its skull to snap, piercing its insides. The creature makes a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the target takes {@damage 8d10} Piercing damage and has the {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB} condition until the end of its next turn. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage only. A creature without a skull is immune to this spell.

spell.name: Divine Order: Reveal
entries: You attempt to gaze upon the soul of a creature within range. The creature makes a Charisma saving throw. On a failure, its origins are revealed to you. You learn the creature's alignment and creature type and all of its ability scores, saving throw proficiencies, skill proficiencies, weapon proficiencies, armor training, senses, languages, Vulnerabilities, Resistances, Immunities, {@variantrule Challenge Rating|XPHB}, and traits.
If a creature succeeds on this saving throw, it is immune to this spell for the next 24 hours.
GM Note: This is the equivalent of revealing the upper part (above the actions) of a stat block to your player.

spell.name: Forest of Dread
entries: You touch the ground beneath yourself, and tree-like bones sprout from the ground in a 30-foot-radius, 20-foot-high {@variantrule Cylinder [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cylinder} centered on you. Each creature of your choice in that area must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 4d8} Piercing damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

For the duration, the area becomes {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}, and when a creature moves into or within the area, it takes {@damage 2d8} Piercing damage for every 5 feet it travels; you are immune to this effect. The forest of dread provides {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Half Cover} to any creatures inside the area. The bones disintegrate when the spell ends.

spell.name: Starfall
entries: You create a violent star shower. When you cast this spell and as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} on your subsequent turns, you can bring down stars on creatures of your choice in 30-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} centered on you. Each creature struck by a star takes {@damage 3d4} Radiant damage.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d4} for each spell slot level above 5.

spell.name: Erupting Lightning

components.m: the vertebrae of a Lightning Vessel barbarian

entries: You slam the ground with the palm of your hand, causing pillars of lightning to erupt from the ground beneath your foes. The area of lightning consists of up to seven 5-foot Cubes, which you can arrange as you wish. Each {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cube} must have at least one face adjacent to the face of another {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cube}. Each creature in the area makes a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes {@damage 7d10} Lightning damage and has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition. On a successful save, a target takes half as much damage only.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: You generate two additional 5-foot Cubes of lightning for each spell slot level above 6.

spell.name: Unbound Chamber
entries: Using a ritual of ancient magic, you distort the gravitational balance of an enclosed space of your choice for the duration. The chamber can be of any shape up to 200 feet wide, 200 feet high, and 200 feet long. While in the chamber, a creature doesn't take damage from falling, has its jump distance tripled, has a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} equal to its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}, and can hover.
Casting this spell on the same spot every day for a year makes this effect permanent.

spell.name: Void Walk
entries: You touch a willing creature. For the duration, it gains a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} equal to its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} and can hover.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: You can target one additional creature for each spell slot level above 6.

spell.name: Wall of Bones
entries: A grim wall of fractured bones forms at a point you choose within range. The wall appears in any orientation you choose: horizontally, vertically, or diagonally. It must rest on a solid surface. The wall can be up to 60 feet long, 10 feet high, and 5 feet thick. The wall blocks line of sight, and creatures can't pass through it. The wall is an object and has AC 15, 150 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to Poison and Psychic damage, and {@variantrule Vulnerability|XPHB} to Bludgeoning damage. Reducing it to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} destroys it.

When the wall appears, each creature in its space is pushed out of it by the shortest route and must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes {@damage 4d8} Bludgeoning damage. On a successful save, it takes half as much damage.

Until the spell ends, and as long as the wall has sufficient {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to sacrifice 20 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} from the wall to summon a {@creature Minotaur Skeleton|XMM} that has murderous killing intent. The creature appears in an unoccupied space of your choice adjacent to the wall. When you summon a minotaur skeleton in this way, designate a creature you can see within 60 feet of it; that creature becomes the sole enemy of the summoned creature. The minotaur skeleton takes its turn immediately after yours, ignores any orders you give, tries to kill its target by any means, and has {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition. Once its target is dead, it vanishes in a pile of dust.
You can have a maximum of three creatures summoned by this spell at a time.

spell.name: Amputate
entries: You coat your hand with vicious Osteomancy. Make a melee spell attack against a creature within your reach. On a hit, you deal {@damage 10d8} Slashing damage and rip off one of the creature's limbs (leg, arm, or other similar appendage).

A creature is immune to this effect if it has {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to Slashing damage, if it has Legendary Actions, or if the GM decides that the creature is too big for its limbs to be cut off with this spell. Such a creature takes an extra {@damage 5d8} Slashing damage instead.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The initial damage increases by {@dice 4d8} and the extra damage increases by {@dice 2d8} for each spell slot level above 7.

spell.name: Boneyard

m.text: enchanted bones, incense, and herbs worth at least 1000+ GP, which the spell consumes

entries: You touch a point and infuse a {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} around it with Osteomancy. The {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} can have a radius of up to 120 feet, and the spell fails if the {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} includes an area already under the effect of a Boneyard or {@spell Hallow|XPHB} spell. The affected area is subject to the following effects.

entries.name: Dead Man's Tale
entries: You know the exact location and hear the thoughts (if any) of any Undead within the area, and you know if they are under someone's control.

entries.name: Early Grave
entries: Any creatures within the area lose {@spell Resistance|XPHB} and {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to Necrotic and Piercing damage.

entries.name: Funeral March
entries: You can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} whenever a non-Undead creature dies within the area, ripping its skeleton from its flesh and forcing it to rise again under your control. If you do, the creature is resurrected with 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point}, its creature type becomes Undead, it has {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to all damage, and it loses access to any Legendary Actions it had (if any). It takes its turn immediately after yours, and you can mentally command its actions. At the end of its next turn, it dies and can't be resurrected by any means short of a {@spell Wish|XPHB} spell.

spell.name: Crush
entries: You attempt to trap one creature you can see within range in a field of gravity that compresses and folds in on itself. The target must succeed on a Strength saving throw or take {@damage 12d10} Bludgeoning damage and have the {@condition Stunned|XPHB} condition until the start of your next turn as it reels from the pain.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: You can target one additional creature for each spell slot level above 7.

spell.name: Divine Order: Transcend
entries: You call upon the power of the Great Ones, seeking their guidance. Until the spell ends, you can cast any spell from the Warlock spell list of level 5 or lower without expending a spell slot or needing to prepare the spell. As part of casting this spell, you can cast a spell with a casting time of an action from your newfound spell list. However, the cost of using this power is steep. At the end of each of your turns, you must succeed on a DC 18 Intelligence saving throw or take {@damage 2d10} Psychic damage. If you fail this saving throw two times, the spell ends early and you gain a Short- Term Madness.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: When you cast this spell using a level 9 spell slot, it doesn't require {@status Concentration|XPHB}, and the duration becomes 1 minute. You can choose to end the spell early (no action required).

spell.name: Bury
entries: You dramatically increase the pull of gravity in a 30-foot- radius, 300-foot-high {@variantrule Cylinder [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cylinder} centered on a point on the ground within range. Creatures in this area have the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition and can't lose this condition while there. A creature that enters the area for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition and takes {@damage 4d6} Bludgeoning damage. A flying creature in the area immediately falls to the ground and has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition. A creature must succeed on a Strength saving throw to move or perform any action. Each foot of movement while in the area costs a creature 3 extra feet.

A {@condition Prone|XPHB} creature that ends its turn in the {@variantrule Cylinder [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cylinder} must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be forced into the ground as gravity pulls it down. A creature forced into the ground has the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} and {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} conditions from the crushing pressure, but it gains the benefits of {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Three-Quarters Cover}, as only the upper part of their body is visible. A creature repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, freeing itself from the ground on a success.

If the spell lasts for its full duration, the ground cracks under the tremendous weight generated and becomes {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}. Each 5-foot-square area requires at least 1 minute to clear by hand to remove the {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}.

spell.name: Aspect of Death

m.text: the skull of a fallen hero, embedded with jewels worth at least 4,000 GP+

entries: You focus threads of magical energy around you that coalesce into the aspect of death, an avatar made of bones, which surrounds you and fights on your behalf. The avatar is Huge, is centered on you, and moves with you. No other creature can enter the space of the avatar. While it is summoned, it protects you from foes; you are considered behind {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Three-Quarters Cover}, have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to all damage, and have {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition.

In addition, you gain a +1 bonus to spell attack rolls and saving throws, and when you cast a spell that deals Bludgeoning, Piercing, or Slashing damage, it is cast with a spell slot 2 levels higher than the one expended (to a maximum of level 9).

spell.name: Divine Order: Sacrifice
entries: When you cast this spell, you create a crack in the world, letting the will of a Great One smother a creature of your choice within range. The direct contact with the eldritch being causes the target to take {@damage 8d6} Psychic damage, and it makes a Charisma saving throw. On a failed save, the target is pulled in through the tear to the cosmos. On a successful save, the target gains a Long-Term Madness, and the spell ends.

While in the cosmos, the target has the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition. At the start of each of its turns while there, the target takes {@damage 3d6} Cold damage plus {@damage 3d6} Psychic damage, and it must succeed on a DC 20 Intelligence saving throw or gain a Short-Term Madness.

If the target is still alive when the spell ends, it reappears in the space it left or in the nearest unoccupied space if that space is occupied. If the target is killed while in the void, its corpse never returns. A body that disappears in this way can never be recovered, not even with a {@spell Wish|XPHB} spell.
If your {@status Concentration|XPHB} on the spell ends early, you must succeed on a DC 20 Intelligence saving throw or gain a Long-Term Madness as the eldritch whispers revolt and assault your mind.
When the spell ends, roll a {@dice d100}. On a 1, the breach doesn't close and instead only expands, threatening to absorb the entire world.

spell.name: Osteophagia
entries: This spell is the most lethal osteomantic power. You can cause the skeleton of a creature to liquefy in a matter of seconds and its body to collapse in on itself. Choose a creature within your reach; it must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the target dies. On a successful save, the target takes {@damage 10d6} Necrotic damage. A creature without bones or that has a {@variantrule Challenge Rating|XPHB} or character level equal to or higher than the caster's {@variantrule Challenge Rating|XPHB} or character level is immune to this spell.

sizeEntry.name: Size:
entries: Small (2-4 feet tall) or Medium (about 4-7 feet tall)

entries.name: Age
entries: As they are not alive, Manikins do not age. However, their materials slowly lose their magic essence over time, meaning they will ultimately lose control over their entire body and turn back into inanimate objects.

entries.name: Born to Serve
entries: Manikins are made to be unable to hate or resent their creators. This lapse in judgment affects you in all circumstances. You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Insight|XPHB}) checks.

entries.name: Electric Heart
entries: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Lightning damage.

entries.name: Integrated Gold Plating
entries: Your body has built-in defensive layers. You gain no AC benefit from wearing armor, but if you are wielding a {@item Shield|XPHB}, you apply its bonus as normal. When you select this species, choose one of the following armor presets:

items.name: Infiltrator
entries: Your base {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} equals 11 plus your Dexterity modifier. You are considered unarmored for the purpose of features that require you to not be wearing any armor. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth|XPHB}) checks.

items.name: Sentinel
entries: Your base {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} equals 13 plus either your Dexterity modifier (maximum of 2) or your Strength modifier (maximum of 3). You are considered to be wearing Medium armor. If you don't have training in Medium armor, you suffer the associated drawbacks.

items.name: Tormentor
entries: Your base {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} equals 16 plus your Strength modifier (maximum of 2). You are considered to be wearing Heavy armor. If you don't have training in Heavy armor, you suffer the associated drawbacks. You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth|XPHB}) checks.

entries.name: Living Material
entries: You don't need to eat, drink, or breathe and have {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition.

entries.name: Service Model
entries: You've been created for a specific purpose. Choose one of the following benefits when you select this species:

items.name: Custodian
entries: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on any ability check you make to end the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition. You also count as one size larger when determining your carrying capacity. Additionally, when a creature within 5 feet of you is targeted by an attack, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to jump in front of it. If the creature is willing, you switch places with it, becoming the target of the attack instead. You can use this {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

items.name: Handler
entries: You gain proficiency in the {@skill Stealth|XPHB} skill and with the {@item Disguise Kit|XPHB}. Additionally, you can have up to two Melee weapons embedded within your body. Each weapon must have either the {@itemProperty F|XPHB|Finesse} or {@itemProperty L|XPHB|Light} property. Over the course of 1 hour, which can be done during a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}, you can remove one or both weapons and exchange them for other appropriate weapons you are holding. You can draw or stow these embedded weapons as you would normal weapons. You can't be disarmed of your embedded weapons, short of having your arms cut off.

items.name: Thespian
entries: You gain proficiency in the {@skill Performance|XPHB} skill. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can form a connection between yourself and a willing creature you can see within 30 feet via a set of immaterial strings for 1 hour. If the creature you're connected to doesn't use all of its movement on its turn (if it has multiple {@variantrule Speed|XPHB|Speeds}, pick the highest one), you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} at the end of its turn to move a number of feet equal to its remaining movement. Once you form a connection in this way, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

sizeEntry.name: Size:
entries: Medium (about 4-7 feet tall)

speedEntry.name: Speed:
entries: 30 feet, {@variantrule Climb Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}

entries.name: Blood Moon's Call
entries: If a Blood Moon is out while you are asleep, you have a dream. In this dream, the Black Goat places a compulsion within your mind, ordering you to carry out some activity as a torrent of images and sensations. When you awaken, you are affected by the {@spell Suggestion|XPHB} spell (save DC 16) and must carry out the order. On a successful save, you resist the spell.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You have {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} with a range of 60 feet.

entries.name: Eldritch Endurance
entries: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Necrotic damage.

entries.name: Legacy of a Thousand Young
entries: You are the recipient of a legacy that grants you supernatural abilities. You gain the level 1 benefit of the table below. When you reach character levels 3 and 5, you learn a higher-level spell, as shown on the table. You always have that spell prepared. You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for the spells you cast with this trait (choose the ability when you select the species).

sizeEntry.name: Size:
entries: Small (about 2-4 feet tall)

speedEntry.name: Speed:
entries: 25 feet, {@variantrule Climb Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}. You can't use your {@variantrule Climb Speed|XPHB} while you wear Medium or Heavy armor.

entries.name: Age
entries: Cursed-bloods develop much faster than humans, reaching maturity after 5 years, and have shorter lifespans, passing after roughly 60 years, though some rare individuals have lived up to 100.

entries.name: Together Forever
entries: Your dead twin manifests within your stomach in supernatural ways. Choose a Twin Manifestation from the Twin Manifestations table. You gain the level 1 benefit of that manifestation. When you reach character levels 3 and 5, you gain further abilities. If you learn a spell as part of this trait, you always have that spell prepared. You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for the spells you cast with this trait (choose the ability when you select the twin).

entries.caption: Twin Manifestations
rows: Your stomach is bigger on the inside than on the outside. It can hold up to 250 pounds, not exceeding a volume of 32 cubic feet. If you die, you harmlessly regurgitate all objects devoured. Your stomach holds enough air for a creature to breathe 1 minute inside it. Nonmagical items inside your stomach are destroyed by psychic energies after 24 hours.
Choose one damage type from among Acid, Cold, Fire, or Poison. Whenever you take damage of that type, you reduce it by an amount equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}.
Nonmagical items inside your stomach are no longer destroyed by psychic energies. In addition, your stomach can hold up to 500 pounds and has a maximum volume of 64 cubic feet.

You have telepathy with a range of 5 times your character level. A creature without telepathy that receives your telepathic messages can't communicate mentally to you. You also know the {@spell Mage Hand|XPHB} cantrip.
You can turn a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} scored against you into a normal hit. Once you use this benefit, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}.
A creature without telepathy that receives your telepathic messages can communicate with you using a language it knows.
You have {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} with a range of 120 feet. You also learn the {@spell Cosmic Eye|SGttEH24} cantrip.
You also learn the {@spell Detect Magic|XPHB} spell.
You learn the {@spell See Invisibility|XPHB} spell.

entries.name: Vigilant
entries: You can't be {@status surprised|XPHB}. When you roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB}, you can add your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} to the roll.

sizeEntry.name: Size:
entries: Medium (about 6-7 feet tall)

entries.name: Age
entries: Demidritchs mature faster than humans, reaching adulthood after 10 years. Their eldritch powers cause their body to decay much faster, they can live up to 60 years old.

entries.name: Astral Sight
entries: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against the Blinded condition. You also know the {@spell Cosmic Eye|SGttEH24} cantrip. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for it.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You have {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} with a range of 120 feet.

entries.name: Shard of Infinity
entries: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Cold damage.

entries.name: Unleash Infinity
entries: When you reach character level 3, you can transform as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} using one of the options below (choose the option when you select this species). The transformation lasts for 1 minute or until you end it (no action required). Once you transform, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. Here are the transformation options:

items.name: Nebulare
items.entry: You explode with eldritch radiance. Creatures other than your allies within 10 feet of you must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw (DC 8 plus your Charisma modifier and {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}) or take a number of d6s of Radiant damage equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}. You shed {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 10-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 10 feet. A 20 foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from you is considered {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB} for your enemies that you can see. When you reach character level 12, the size of this {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} increases to 60 feet, you can gain a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} of 30 feet, and you can hover.

items.name: Oculare
items.entry: No creature within 60 feet of you can have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against you as a result of having the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition. When you reach character level 12, you also grow eye-covered eldritch wings during this transformation that grant you a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} of 30 feet and {@sense Truesight|XPHB} out to a range of 60 feet.

sizeEntry.name: Size:
entries: Medium (about 4-7 feet tall)

entries.name: Age
entries: Scourgeborne are made not born. This means that they mature at the same rate as the race they originally belonged to.

entries.name: Born of Madness
entries: Your body is not the one you were born with, but rather the consequence to your exposure to eldritch madness. You have one of the following options based on your alignment:

items.name: Good Alignment
entries: Your conviction grants you control over your darkest impulses. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws you make to avoid or end Madness but have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Charisma ({@skill Intimidation|XPHB}) checks.

items.name: Neutral Alignment
entries: You do not gain any benefits or penalties from your alignment.

items.name: Evil Alignment
entries: You let the depraved monster within you influence you. You gain a bonus to Dexterity saving throws equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} but have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on saving throws you make to avoid or end Madness.

entries.name: Eldritch Curse
entries: Your curse is not something mere mortals can undo. You are immune to any spell that would alter your form, such as the Alter Self or Polymorph spells.

entries.name: Feral Limbs
entries: You can use your horns, claws, or fangs originating from your mutation to make {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB|Unarmed Strikes}. When you do so, you deal Piercing damage equal to {@dice 1d6} plus your Strength modifier instead of the normal damage for your {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}. If your alignment is Evil, your feral side is more dominant, and the die size for your {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB|Unarmed Strikes} increases to a {@dice d8}.

entries.name: Monstrous Lineage
entries: You are part of a lineage that grants you supernatural abilities. Choose a lineage from the Monstrous Lineages table. You gain the level 1 benefit of that lineage. When you reach character levels 3 and 5, you gain further benefits, as shown on the table.

entries.caption: Lineage
rows: You have a {@variantrule Climb Speed|XPHB} of 30 feet. While in water, you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on all ability checks and attack rolls.

You can climb difficult surfaces, including along ceilings, without needing to make an ability check. Once you use this trait for 1 minute, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
