You can use your Level 3 trait for a number of minutes equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all expended minutes when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Perception|XPHB}) checks that rely on hearing or smell. Whenever you cause a creature to become {@status Bloodied|XPHB}, you take {@dice 1d4} Psychic damage as you attempt to retain your sanity.

As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can make an Unarmed Strike to feed off of a creature within reach. On a hit, you regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the damage dealt. Once you use this trait, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
You can use your Level 3 trait a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases to 40 feet. Standing up from the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition costs you an additional 5 feet.

If you move at least 20 feet straight toward a creature and then hit it with a melee attack on the same turn, that target must make a Strength saving throw (DC 8 plus your Strength modifier and your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}). On a failed save, the target has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition, and you can immediately take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to make one melee attack against it. Once you've forced a creature to make this save, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
You can use your Level 3 trait a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
You have {@sense Blindsight|XPHB} with a range of 30 feet. You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on any ability check or attack roll that requires sight beyond this range.

You can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to gain a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} of 30 feet until the end of your turn. If nothing is holding you aloft at the end of your turn, you fall. Once you use this trait, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

You can use your Level 3 trait a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

sizeEntry.name: Size:
entries: Medium (about 4-5 feet tall)

entries.name: Age
entries: Deep One Dwarves mature much faster than humans, reaching maturity at the age of 10. They cannot die of old age; instead their aging mind progressively loses grip over their body, turning them into brainless servants of He Who Lies Dreaming when they reach 50 years of age.

entries.name: Amphibious
entries: You can breathe air and water.

entries.name: Cosmic Knowledge
entries: Whenever you make an Intelligence ({@skill History|XPHB}) check related to the origin of an eldritch item or construction, you are considered to have proficiency and {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB} in the {@skill History|XPHB} skill.

entries.name: Dreamer's Curse
entries: Your mind is clouded by the dreams of the Slumbering One, and you find it hard to discern between reality and dream. You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on ability checks and saving throws to discern illusions.

entries.name: Guiding Light
entries: You know the {@spell Dancing Lights|XPHB} cantrip. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for it.

entries.name: Illusory Body
entries: Although your body is covered in tentacles, clams, seashells, and other eldritch appendages, you are covered by an illusion that makes you appear like a typical hill dwarf to others. Other Deep Ones can see through this illusion. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Charisma ({@skill Deception|XPHB}) checks against creatures that can't see through this illusion.

entries.name: Otherworldly Resistance
entries: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Cold damage. You also have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws you make to avoid or end the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition.

entries.name: Slumbering Legacy
entries: You are descended from the Slumbering One and receive a supernatural boon from your ancestry. Choose one of the following benefits.

items.name: Coiling Arm
entries: Your arm is nothing but a writhing mass of tentacles that flail in the air. On your turn, immediately after hitting an enemy with a melee attack, you can make an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} using the Grapple option against it as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} (normal grappling restrictions apply).

items.name: Brutal Pincers
entries: Your forearm becomes a massive pincer, which you can use to make {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB|Unarmed Strikes}. If you hit with it, you deal Piercing damage equal to {@dice 1d6} plus your Strength modifier, instead of the Bludgeoning damage normal for an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}. If you hit a creature you are grappling, the damage die becomes a {@dice d10} instead. You can't use this arm to make any precise hand manipulations.

items.name: Mucoid Extremities
entries: Your hands and feet are palmed and covered in cups. Your {@variantrule Swim Speed|XPHB} increases to 35 feet, and you gain a {@variantrule Climb Speed|XPHB} of 20 feet.
{@i {@b OPTIONAL: Dreamer's Gift.}} Your devotion to the cosmic being that warped your species is rewarded. When you reach character level 5, you gain the following benefits:

items: Whenever you fail an ability check or saving throw, you can draw upon your master's powers to succeed instead. Once you use this benefit, you can't do so again until the Dreamer has replaced one of your {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB|D20 Tests} with a 1. The GM decides when this unfortunate moment happens.
You are immune to the Curse of the Slumbering Moon.

optionalfeature.name: Pact of the Trigger
entries: You can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to draw upon the powers of your patron and create a hex gun in your empty hand. Each time you create it, choose either Revolver Form or Sniper Form (detailed below). You can use your hex gun as a {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB} for your Warlock spells.
Your hex gun disappears if it is more than 5 feet away from you for 1 minute. It also disappears if you use this feature again, if you dismiss it (no action required), or if you die.

entries.name: Cantrips
entries: When you cast cantrips with your hex gun, it channels them as bullets (these still count as spells). Instead of increasing the damage of the cantrip when you reach certain levels, you can cast the cantrip additional times as part of the same action (except for {@spell Eldritch Blast|XPHB}, which is cast as normal). To be cast in this way, the cantrip must deal damage, must make a {@filter spell attack|spells|source=|level=0|class=warlock|damage type=acid;bludgeoning;cold;fire;force;lightning;necrotic;piercing;poison;psychic;radiant;slashing;thunder|spell attack=m;r|range=!self;!self (area)} or require a {@filter saving throw|spells|level=0|class=warlock|damage type=acid;bludgeoning;cold;fire;force;lightning;necrotic;piercing;poison;psychic;radiant;slashing;thunder|saving throw=strength;dexterity;constitution;intelligence;wisdom;charisma|range=!self;!self (area)}, and must have a range other than Self. When you cast a cantrip in this manner, you cast it 2 times at level 5, 3 times at level 11, and 4 times at level 17. The cantrip can deal its damage more than once, but any other effects can't be applied again this turn.

entries.name: Revolver Form
entries: While holding your hex gun, you know the {@spell Shocking Grasp|XPHB} cantrip. In addition, being within 5 feet of a hostile creature doesn't impose {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on your ranged spell attack rolls cast through the hex gun.

entries.name: Sniper Form
entries: While holding your hex gun, you know the {@spell Ray of Frost|XPHB} cantrip. In addition, your ranged spell attack rolls cast through the hex gun have their range doubled and ignore {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Half Cover}.

optionalfeature.name: Bullet Hell
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while holding your hex gun and you have one free hand, you temporarily duplicate your hex gun to unleash a flurry of bullets. Each creature in a 15-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone} must succeed a Dexterity saving throw against your spell save DC or take {@damage 2d10} Force damage. This damage increases to {@dice 3d10} at level 11 and {@dice 4d10} at level 17.
You can use this invocation a number of times equal to twice your Charisma modifier (minimum of twice), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

optionalfeature.name: Crippling Shot
entries: Once on each of your turns when you damage a creature with an attack made using your hex gun, you can force it to make a Strength saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the target has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition and its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced to 0 until the start of your next turn.
You can use this invocation a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

optionalfeature.name: Dead Eye
entries: If you haven't moved this turn, you can take aim as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, reducing your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} to 0 and granting you {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attacks you make using your hex gun until the end of your turn.

optionalfeature.name: Headshots
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can enhance your focus to see the weak spots of your foes. For the next minute, attack rolls you make using your hex gun score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} on a roll of 19 or 20 on the {@dice d20}. Once you use this invocation, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

optionalfeature.name: Quickstep
entries: Your reflexes are honed. When a creature makes a melee attack roll against you, if your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} isn't 0, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to move 5 feet away from the creature without triggering {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}, causing the attack to miss if you leave its reach.
You can use this invocation a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

optionalfeature.name: Reckless Fire
entries: Your shots are a rain of lead. When you roll a 1, 2, or 3 on a damage die for a cantrip you cast with your hex gun, you can reroll the die and must use the new roll, even if the new roll is a 1, 2, or 3.

optionalfeature.name: Repeating Cantrips
entries: When you cast a cantrip with your hex gun, its nondamaging effects (such as the {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} reduction of {@spell Ray of Frost|XPHB}) can apply multiple times but not more than once per creature on a turn.

optionalfeature.name: Ricochet
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you enhance your hex gun until the end of your turn. When you hit a creature with an attack roll from a cantrip cast with your hex gun, you can immediately cause the cantrip to wound a second creature within 30 feet of the original target. The second target takes damage (of the type of the cantrip) equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1).

optionalfeature.name: Riposte
entries: When a creature makes a melee attack roll against you while you are holding your hex gun, you can fire a special bullet to counter the attack. Make a melee or ranged spell attack against the attacker. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 1d10} Force damage, the attack misses, and it has the {@condition Stunned|XPHB} condition until the end of its current turn.
Once you use this invocation to damage an enemy, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

optionalfeature.name: Weapon Parry
entries: As a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to being hit by an attack from a creature you can see within range of a weapon you are holding, you can expend 1 Adrenaline Point to reduce the attack's damage. The reduction equals one roll of your weapon's damage dice plus your Strength or Dexterity modifier (your choice) and your Jaeger level.

If you roll the maximum amount of the weapon's damage die and reduce the damage to 0, you can force the attacker to make a Constitution saving throw (DC = 8 + your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} + your Dexterity or Strength modifier, whichever is higher). On a failed save, the target has the {@condition Stunned|XPHB} condition until the start of your next turn.

optionalfeature.name: Dodge Step
entries: When an attack roll hits you, if your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is not 0, you can expend 1 Adrenaline Point to take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} moving 5 feet without provoking {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks} and making a Dexterity saving throw with a DC equal to the attacker's attack roll (including modifiers). On a success, you evade completely and take no damage. On a failure, you halve the attack's damage against you.

optionalfeature.name: Aerial Vault
entries: Once per turn on your turn, when you make a jump, you can expend 1 Adrenaline Point to double your jumping distance for that jump, and you can ignore {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB} until the end of your turn. When you use this Adrenaline Art, the maximum distance you can jump isn't limited by your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}.

optionalfeature.name: Spell Flurry
entries: When you cast a cantrip or a spell of 1st level or higher, you can expend 1 Adrenaline Point to make a single weapon attack as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.

optionalfeature.name: Prayer of Salvation
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you expend 1 Adrenaline Point. You and one creature of your choice you can see within 60 feet of you each regain {@dice 1d6} {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} and gain an equal number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}.

optionalfeature.name: Blood Craze
entries: As a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to being reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} but not killed outright, you can expend 1 Adrenaline Point to fall to 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} instead.

entries.name: Note: Blood Craze
entries: This is a Adrenaline Art, so it works with {@classFeature Always Ready|Jaeger|SGttEH24|20}, (which grants an additional {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}), making the {@subclass Sanguine|Jaeger|SGttEH24|SGttEH24|Sanguine Chapter} fairly difficult to stop at 20th level.

optionalfeature.name: Elemental Art
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you expend 1 Adrenaline Point and touch a weapon you're carrying. Choose between Acid, Cold, Fire, or Lightning. For 1 minute, the weapon deals that damage type instead of its normal type. Once per turn on your turn, when you hit with an attack using this weapon, you deal additional damage equal to one roll of your Momentum Die. The damage is the same type as the one chosen for the weapon.

optionalfeature.name: Focus Mind
entries: As a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to making a saving throw against the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} or {@condition Frightened|XPHB} conditions, or having your mind read or influenced, you can expend 1 Adrenaline Point togain {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the roll. If you already have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the roll, you can reroll one of the dice once.

optionalfeature.name: I Don't Want To Be Eaten Today
entries: As a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to gaining the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} or {@condition Restrained|XPHB} conditions from an effect that has an escape DC or saving throw to end the effect early, you can expend 1 Adrenaline Point to attempt to immediately escape the effect, making an {@skill Athletics|XPHB} or {@skill Acrobatics|XPHB} check against the escape DC or saving throw DC.

optionalfeature.name: Jaeger's Rush
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend 1 Adrenaline Point to take the {@action Dash|XPHB} action.

optionalfeature.name: Jaeger's Assessment
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend 1 Adrenaline Point to make an {@skill Investigation|XPHB} check against a creature you can see within 60 feet of you, contested by its {@skill Deception|XPHB} check. On success, you learn its creature type, AC, any damage {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB|Resistances}, {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB|Immunities}, or {@variantrule Vulnerability|XPHB|Vulnerabilities} it has, and any spells currently affecting it.
Alternatively, when you take this {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can take the {@action Search|XPHB} action.

optionalfeature.name: Brutal
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack, you can expend all of your Momentum dice and add them to the damage roll. If you reduce the target to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} with this Finisher, or the target is reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} before the start of your next turn, you regain 1 Adrenaline Point.

optionalfeature.name: Breaking Blow
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack, you can expend all of your Momentum dice to force the target to make a saving throw, suffering a condition for 1 minute on a failure. The DC of the save is 8 + your Strength or Dexterity modifier (your choice) + your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}. The type of saving throw and the condition depend on the number of Momentum dice you have when you use this Finisher. You can choose to inflict a condition that requires fewer Momentum dice than you expend, but all Momentum dice are expended regardless of the condition selected.

The target can repeat its saving throw against the condition at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. If the condition inflicted is {@condition Prone|XPHB}, they do not need to pass a subsequent save and can end the condition by standing as normal instead. If the target fails its initial save against the effect, you regain 1 Adrenaline Point.

optionalfeature.name: Chasing
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you expend all of your Momentum dice to move 10 feet per die expended before making a melee weapon attack. This movement can be in direction, including straight up. If nothing is holding you aloft after this attack, you fall. On a hit, add the expended Momentum dice to the attack's damage roll, and until the end of the target's next turn, its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced by 10 feet per die expended.
If you move at least 30 feet using the Finisher, you regain 1 Adrenaline Point.

optionalfeature.name: Great Cleave
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack using a weapon with the {@itemProperty 2H|XPHB|Two-Handed} Property, you can expend all of your Momentum dice. When you do so, the attack deals extra damage equal to 1 Momentum die to the target, and you cleave a number of adjacent targets within Reach equal to the number of dice expended. Make a melee weapon attack against each creature targeted. You regain 1 Adrenaline Point for each creature this reduces to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, up to your maximum number of Adrenaline Points.

optionalfeature.name: Blood Drain
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you expend all of your Momentum dice to drain the blood of a creature within 15 feet of you, magically consuming its vitality to empower you. The target must make a Constitution saving throw. Roll all of your expended Momentum dice, on a failed save the target takes Necrotic damage equal to the total rolled, or half as much on a successful save. Success or failure, you regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to half the Necrotic damage dealt. If this would restore you above your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum, you gain the excess as {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}

If the target has the {@status Bloodied|XPHB} condition or is otherwise bleeding from an ability (such as the {@optfeature Hemorrhaging Wound|SGttEH24} Finisher), it automatically fails its saving throw against this finisher, and you regain 1 Adrenaline Point.

optionalfeature.name: Hemorrhaging Wound
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack, you can expend all of your Momentum dice to rend a vicious bleeding wound. At the end of each of the creature's turns, it loses {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the Momentum dice expended. Each time it takes damage from this effect, the number of dice of damage it takes at the end of its next turn is reduced by 1, and the bleeding stops when the number of dice would be reduced to 0.

Applying a new bleed while the target is still bleeding does not stack, but instead refreshes the number of dice of damage taken from the bleed to the higher of the two values. This bleeding lasts for 1 minute or until the target regains {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.
If a creature is slain while bleeding from this effect, you regain 1 Adrenaline Point.
{@i {@b Rules Reminder:} Whenever you add the number of Momentum dice expended to damage, roll the expended dice and add the total rolled to the damage, not the number of dice itself.}

optionalfeature.name: Opportunistic Shot
entries: As a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} when a creature within 30 feet of you becomes {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB}, {@condition Restrained|XPHB}, or {@condition Stunned|XPHB}, you can expend all of your Momentum dice and make a single weapon attack against it with a ranged weapon you are holding. On a hit, the target takes damage equal to the weapon's damage roll plus the expended Momentum dice, you can inflict the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition on the target, and you regain 1 Adrenaline Point.

optionalfeature.name: Vicious
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you expend all of your Momentum dice to warp your hand into a beast claw and make a melee weapon attack with it against a creature within 5 feet of you. You can use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls of this attack. On a hit, the target takes Slashing damage equal to {@dice 1d12} + your Strength or Dexterity modifier + the Momentum dice expended. This attack has a higher {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} range based on the number of Momentum dice expended, reducing the roll needed by 2 for each die, up to a maximum of scoring a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} on a 10-20 with 5 Momentum dice.
If the Finisher is a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB}, you regain 1 Adrenaline Point.

optionalfeature.name: Volley
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while you are holding a firearm, you can expend all of your Momentum dice to reload and fire a spray of shots at blinding speed. Each creature in a 30 foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone} must make a Dexterity saving throw (DC = 8 + your Dexterity modifier + your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}). On a failure, a creature takes Piercing damage equal to the Momentum dice expended.
If the Finisher damages 2 or more creatures, you regain 1 Adrenaline Point.

item.name: Scatter Bullets (10)
entries: When you make an attack using a weapon with the {@itemProperty SGttEH:SpFr|SGttEH24|Spread Fire} property, you can choose to use this ammunition. If you do, ignore the {@itemProperty SGttEH:SpFr|SGttEH24|Spread Fire} property and instead make an attack roll. On a hit, the target takes the weapon's normal damage, and the bullet explodes, sending shrapnel flying in a 15-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone} originating from the target and extending away from you. Each creature in the area must make a Dexterity saving throw (DC equals 8 plus your Dexterity modifier plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}). On a failed save, a creature takes damage equal to your weapon damage.
Once it hits a target, the ammunition is destroyed.

item.name: Explosive Bullets (10)
entries: A creature hit by this ammunition takes the weapon's normal damage, and each creature within 5 feet of it must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw (DC 8 plus the ability modifier used to make the attack roll and your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}) or take {@damage 1d8} Fire damage. On a miss, the ammunition fails to detonate.
Once it hits a target, the ammunition is destroyed.

item.name: Bag of Cannonballs
entries: This bag connects to an extradimensional space that allows it to hold numerous items while never weighing more than 3 pounds. It can hold up to 100 {@item Cannonball|SGttEH24|Cannonballs} or similar objects. You can take out {@item Cannonball|SGttEH24|Cannonballs} as if doing so from a normal sack (no action required).

item.name: Damascena
entries: This basic technology invented by the Scions sacrifices lead bullets to generate powerful azure flames that scorch the abhorrent. This device is an effort to replicate the magical ability possessed by a select few.

As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can cause the Damascena to project blue flames in a 15-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone}. Each creature in the {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone} must make a Dexterity saving throw (DC 11 plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}), taking {@damage 4d4} Fire damage plus a number of d4s equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. Each use of the Damascena consumes 5 {@item Bullet|SGttEH24|Bullets}.

item.name: Hand of  Glory
entries: A hand of glory is crafted from the hand of a hanged scourgeborne after it was covered in corpse wax and left to ferment in a casket.
While holding the hand, you can attune to one additional magical item. This magical item can only be a ring and must be worn on the Hand of Glory.

This hand has 5 charges and regains 1 charge daily at dusk. Whenever you expend a charge, a finger from the hand is burnt to a stump. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action while holding the hand, you can expend 1 charge and set a digit of the hand ablaze to cast the {@spell Invisibility|XPHB} spell targeting yourself. For the duration of the spell, the hand sheds {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 10-foot radius that is only visible to the wearer of the hand. The spell ends early if the flame is extinguished by the wearer (no action required).
In addition, while under the effects of the {@spell Invisibility|XPHB} spell, you can cast the {@spell Knock|XPHB} spell at will on objects within the radius of the {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB}.

entries.name: Charged Shot
entries: Once on each of your turns when you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action, you can replace one of your Cannon attacks with a charged shot. Instead of a regular attack, you let loose a ball of destruction and death. Each creature in a 120-foot-long, 5-foot-wide {@variantrule Line [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Line} in front of you must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw (DC 8 plus your Strength modifier and your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}) or take {@damage 3d12} Bludgeoning damage and have the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.
You can use this property only if you haven't moved during your turn, and after you use the property, your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is 0 until the end of your turn.

item.name: Big Bertha
entries: This moongold-inlaid Cannon deals an extra {@damage 1d4} Bludgeoning damage to any non-Humanoid creatures it hits. Moongold acts as a guiding light in the darkness; creatures holding or wearing moongold have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition and against Madness.
You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can rotate the dials on the cannon to infuse your loaded ammo with violent powers. Your next attack gains one of the following benefits:

items.name: Forceful Infusion
items.entry: On a hit, the target takes an extra {@damage 1d6} Bludgeoning damage and must succeed on a DC 15 Strength saving throw or be pushed 15 feet away and have the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition. Alternatively, if you are standing on a solid surface and instead fire the Cannon directly at your feet, the explosion pushes you into the air, and you land in a space of your choice within 30 feet of the explosion's origin.

items.name: Incendiary Infusion
items.entry: On a hit, the target takes an extra {@damage 3d4} Fire damage.

items.name: Unstable Infusion
items.entry: On a hit, the target takes an extra {@damage 1d8} Cold damage, and the terrain in a 10-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the target is covered in arcane mist, becoming {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB} for the next minute.

baseitem.name: Explosive Bullet
entries: A creature hit by this ammunition takes the weapon's normal damage, and each creature within 5 feet of it must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw (DC 8 plus the ability modifier used to make the attack roll and your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}) or take {@damage 1d8} Fire damage. On a miss, the ammunition fails to detonate.
Once it hits a target, the ammunition is destroyed.

baseitem.name: Explosive Cannonball
entries: A creature hit by this ammunition takes the weapon's normal damage, and each creature within 10 feet of it must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw (DC 8 plus your Strength modifier and your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}) or take {@damage 2d8} Fire damage. On a miss, the ammunition fails to detonate.
Once it hits a target, the ammunition is destroyed.

baseitem.name: Scatter Bullet
entries: When you make an attack using a weapon with the {@itemProperty SGttEH:SpFr|SGttEH24|Spread Fire} property, you can choose to use this ammunition. If you do, ignore the {@itemProperty SGttEH:SpFr|SGttEH24|Spread Fire} property and instead make an attack roll. On a hit, the target takes the weapon's normal damage, and the bullet explodes, sending shrapnel flying in a 15-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone} originating from the target and extending away from you. Each creature in the area must make a Dexterity saving throw (DC equals 8 plus your Dexterity modifier plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}). On a failed save, a creature takes damage equal to your weapon damage.
Once it hits a target, the ammunition is destroyed.

magicvariant.name: Moongold Weapon
entries: Some monsters are resilient to physical damage but they remain susceptible to moongilded weapons, so cautious adventurers invest extra coin to plate their weapons, magical or not, with moongold. Moongold gives a {@damage 1d4} bonus to damage rolls against nonhumanoid creatures. Moongold acts as a guiding light in the darkness; creatures holding or wearing moongold have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition and Madness.

You can moongild a single weapon or ten pieces of ammunition for 1000 gp. This cost represents not only the price of the moongold, but the time and expertise needed to add moongold to the weapon without making it less effective. Moongold loses its effectiveness over time; if a moongilded weapon has not been recoated in moongold after 3 months, the coating falls off, ending its effects. Recoating a weapon before it loses its moongilding costs 500 gp and refreshes the duration of the moongold.

entries.name: Moongold
entries: Moongold gives a {@damage 1d4} bonus to damage rolls against nonhumanoid creatures. Moongold acts as a guiding light in the darkness; creatures holding or wearing moongold have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition and Madness.

magicvariant.name: Moongold Ammunition
entries: Some monsters are resilient to physical damage but they remain susceptible to moongilded weapons, so cautious adventurers invest extra coin to plate their weapons, magical or not, with moongold. Moongold gives a {@damage 1d4} bonus to damage rolls against nonhumanoid creatures. Moongold acts as a guiding light in the darkness; creatures holding or wearing moongold have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition and Madness.

You can moongild a single weapon or ten pieces of ammunition for 1000 gp. This cost represents not only the price of the moongold, but the time and expertise needed to add moongold to the weapon without making it less effective. Moongold loses its effectiveness over time; if a moongilded weapon has not been recoated in moongold after 3 months, the coating falls off, ending its effects. Recoating a weapon before it loses its moongilding costs 500 gp and refreshes the duration of the moongold.

entries.name: Moongold
entries: Moongold gives a {@damage 1d4} bonus to damage rolls against nonhumanoid creatures. Moongold acts as a guiding light in the darkness; creatures holding or wearing moongold have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition and Madness.

entries.name: Artillery
entries: You must use Strength instead of Dexterity for the attack rolls and damage rolls of a weapon with the Artillery property, and you can use the weapon to make a ranged attack only if you have ammunition to fire from it. The type of ammunition required is specified with the weapon's range. Each attack expends one piece of ammunition. Weapons with the Artillery property must be reloaded with an action. After a fight, you can spend 1 minute to recover half the ammunition (round down) you used in the fight; the rest is lost.

entries.name: Barrel
entries: A Barrel weapon can be fired a number of times equal to the number of bullets its barrel can hold (written as Barrel X) before it must be reloaded. It can be reloaded as either an action or instead of an attack as part of the {@action Attack|XPHB} action. If the weapon also has the {@itemProperty L|XPHB|Light} property, it can also be reloaded as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. You must have one free hand to reload the weapon.

entries.name: Blaring
entries: Firing a Blaring weapon makes a thunderous crack. The sound can be heard from up to 100 feet away. Making a ranged attack using a weapon with this property makes it hard to go undetected; you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth|XPHB}) checks made as part of the {@action Hide|XPHB} action until the start of your next turn. In addition, if a creature is within the area of the sound blast, it has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Perception|XPHB}) checks made against you as part of the {@action Search|XPHB} action.

entries.name: Booming
entries: The deflagration from firing a Booming weapon makes a stupendous booming sound. The sound can be heard from up to 500 feet away. Making a ranged attack using a weapon with this property makes it impossible to go undetected; you automatically fail Dexterity ({@skill Stealth|XPHB}) checks made as part of the {@action Hide|XPHB} action until the start of your next turn. In addition, if a creature is within the area of the sound blast, it automatically succeeds on Wisdom ({@skill Perception|XPHB}) checks made against you as part of the {@action Search|XPHB} action.

entries.name: Fan Fire
entries: You can quickly fire this weapon by slamming the hammer repeatedly. As an action, you fire a number of bullets in a row equal to your Dexterity modifier (minimum of 2, up to the entirety of the barrel). Each attack after the first suffers a cumulative -3 penalty to the attack roll. You cannot add any modifier to the damage roll of these attacks beyond the first (such as the bonus from your Dexterity modifier or a magic weapon's bonus to damage rolls). When you take this action, your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} becomes 0 until the end of your turn, and attacks you make using the long range of the weapon automatically miss.

If you roll a 1 on any of the attack rolls, the weapon jams and can't be used again until it is repaired. You can take an action to make a DC 15 Dexterity ({@skill Sleight of Hand|XPHB}) check, repairing the weapon on a success. Alternatively, you can spend 1 hour mending it to automatically succeed on the check. If your check fails, the weapon is broken and must be mended in a specialist's store at a quarter of the cost of the firearm.

entries.name: Steady
entries: A weapon with this property can be stabilized for greater accuracy. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you give yourself a bonus to your next attack roll with this weapon this turn equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and attacking at the weapon's long range doesn't impose {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the attack roll. You can take this {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} only if you haven't moved during your turn, and after you take the {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is halved until the end of your turn.

entries.name: Spread Fire
entries: A weapon that has the Spread Fire property can't make a normal attack. It instead sprays a {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone} with a length equal to its range (for example, a {@item Blunderbuss|SGttEH24} with a range of 20 feet will fire in a 20-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone}). Each creature in the area must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw (DC equals 8 plus your Dexterity modifier and your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}) or take the weapon's normal damage.

entries.name: Twinned Barrel
entries: A Twinned Barrel weapon has two barrels that can each hold a {@item Bullet|SGttEH24} and can both be loaded as part of reloading the weapon. You can fire both barrels of a Twinned Barrel weapon as part of an attack, instead of each barrel individually. Doing so empties both barrels of the weapon (as per the {@itemProperty SGttEH:Bar|SGttEH24|Barrel} property) and increases the damage die of the weapon on a hit (from {@dice d4} to {@dice d6} to {@dice d8} to {@dice d10} to {@dice d12}).
Your foe is well equipped, well trained, battle-hardened. He believes his blasphemous god is on his side. Let him believe what he will. We have the jaegers on ours.

entries.name: GM Note:
entries: This subclass can be played using crossbows instead of bows. It can also be played using firearms, although not all of them. The firearms that can be used for this subclass are: {@item flintlock|SGttEH24|flintlocks}, {@item pistol|SGttEH24|pistols}, {@item rifle|SGttEH24|rifles}.
We are few, and our enemies many, but for each one of us that falls, we shall bring hundreds of them to the grave.
"Do you see these beasts?"
"Yes ma'am."
"I don't want to."
"Yes ma'am."
entries.by: Vicar Priscilla to Steinhardt during the 3rd hunt

entries: Nobles have their honor,
Hunters have their courage,
Priests have their faith,
But all we have is rage.

Even while her body was falling apart, she was still fighting. Even when her arm was being torn to shreds, she was still fighting. When all her allies fell, she stood and kept fighting. Only when the battle was won did she draw her last breath, but she was still standing. Not even death could break her.
entries.by: Unknown survivor of the 26th Eldritch hunt

entries: The sweet smell of blood—oh, it's enough to make a man go mad.

entries.name: Disclaimer:
entries: Please be advised that while some may use torture as a plot device or as a means of gathering information, it is a sensitive and disturbing topic. While the use of torture can be integral to the story being told, which is why it is included in this book, it is important to ensure that everyone at the table comes to an agreement regarding its use prior to the session beginning, and if it's included, whether descriptions should fade to black or be more detailed.

entries.name: GM Note:
entries: The unfair treatment that some fire dancers have received in their life has created deep resentment, warping their pure nature and the flames they wield. These fire dancers wield a dark flame, said to devour life. If you wish to play such a character, replace any mention of fire damage, resistance, or immunity in the subclass abilities with necrotic damage, resistance, or immunity, respectively, and you can choose not to gain the {@subclassFeature Flames of Redemption|Monk|PHB|Fire Dancer|SGttEH24|11|SGttEH24} feature.

subclassFeature.name: Absolute Chapter
entries: {@i Jaegers of the Absolute Chapter aim for the perfect hunt: to kill their prey without suffering a single hit. Quick hands, quick feet, and quick minds, they never stay still—always one step ahead, always striking with lethal accuracy. They are masters of their craft, refining the core techniques of a jaeger to absolute perfection.}

subclassFeature.name: Counter Strike
entries: When you use the {@optfeature Weapon Parry|SGttEH24} Adrenaline Art, if you reduce the damage to 0, you can make a single weapon attack against the attacker if they are in the Reach or range of a weapon you are wielding. If the target is {@condition Stunned|XPHB} as a result of your {@optfeature Weapon Parry|SGttEH24} Adrenaline Art, on a hit, this attack is a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB}.
Whenever you expend an Adrenaline Point on an Adrenaline Art, you gain {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on your next weapon attack roll before the end of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Unencumbered Movement
entries: While you are wearing light armor or no armor, and are not wearing a {@item Shield|XPHB}, your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 10 feet, {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks} against you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB}, and the distance you can move when using the {@optfeature Dodge Step|SGttEH24} Adrenaline Art increases by 5 feet.

subclassFeature.name: Encircling Strike
entries: Once per turn, if you move to the opposite side of a creature from where you started your turn, or are directly on the opposite side of a creature from an allied creature of yours, you deal extra damage to the target equal to your Momentum die the first time you hit it with a melee weapon attack, and you gain 1 Momentum die.

subclassFeature.name: Mobile Pursuer
entries: When you move as part of your {@classFeature Hunter's Pursuit|Jaeger|SGttEH24|6} feature, or {@optfeature Dodge Step|SGttEH24} and {@optfeature Jaeger's Rush|SGttEH24} Adrenaline Arts, you ignore {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}, can move through hostile creatures, and don't need to spend extra movement to climb or swim.

subclassFeature.name: The Hunt
entries: You can declare a hunt (no action required). For 1 minute, you gain the following benefits:

items: You are under the effect of {@spell Freedom of Movement|XPHB}
Each time you expend an Adrenaline Point, you gain 2 Momentum dice instead of 1.
If you start your turn with no Adrenaline Points, you regain 1 Adrenaline Point.

entries: This effect ends early if you become {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB}. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also restore your use of it by expending 4 Adrenaline Points (no action required).

subclassFeature.name: Heretic Chapter
entries: {@i The Heretic Chapter's jaegers are despised by both religious organizations and their adherents. In a world where acts of faith are required to harness divine powers, these jaegers instead rip these powers from the very gods, tearing away their divine fabric. Through blasphemous rituals, strange ingestions, and direct grafting of powers onto their flesh, heretics bind the powers of greater beings to their will. This is a path that calls for a certain madness that is only found in the most ardent of hunters, those who feel the burning need to reach the pinnacle of power and will do anything to achieve it. Some seek such power for its own sake, but most want it to put an end to the nightmare.}

entries.name: Eldritch Invocation
entries: You learn one Eldritch Invocation of your choice from the Warlock's list of {@filter Eldritch Invocation Options|optionalfeatures|feature type=ei}. If an invocation refers to Charisma, you use your Intelligence instead.
At higher levels, you learn additional Eldritch Invocations, and your effective Warlock level for invocation prerequisites increases, as shown below.

items.name: Level 7:
items.entry: You learn one additional Eldritch Invocation. You are considered a level 2 Warlock for invocation prerequisites.

items.name: Level 14:
items.entry: You learn one additional Eldritch Invocation. You are considered a level 5 Warlock for invocation prerequisites.

items.name: Level 17:
items.entry: You learn one additional Eldritch Invocation.

entries: Whenever you gain a level in the Jaeger class, you can replace one Eldritch Invocation you know with another one for which you meet the prerequisites.

entries.name: Spell Flurry
entries: You also learn the {@optfeature Spell Flurry|SGttEH24} Adrenaline Art. This does not count against your total Adrenaline Arts known.

subclassFeature.name: Heretic Magic
entries: You bend dark powers to your service through blasphemous occult rituals, making them your own. You gain the ability to cast spells.

entries.name: Cantrips
entries: You learn two cantrips of your choice from the Warlock spell list. You learn an additional Warlock cantrip of your choice at level 10.

entries.name: Spell Slots
entries: The Heretic Spellcasting table shows how many spell slots you have, as well as the level of those spell slots. All of your spell slots are of the same level. To cast one of your warlock spells of level 1 or higher, you must expend a spell slot. You regain all expended spell slots when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Prepared Spells of Level 1+
entries: You prepare the list of level 1+ spells that are available for you to cast with this feature. To start, choose two level 1 spells from the Warlock spell list.

The number of spells on your list increases as you gain Jaeger levels, as shown in the Prepared Spells column of the Heretic Spellcasting table. Whenever that number increases, choose additional spells from the Warlock spell list until the number of spells on your list matches the number on the table. The chosen spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots. For example, if you're a level 7 Jaeger, your list of prepared spells can include five Warlock spells of levels 1 and 2 in any combination.

entries.name: Changing your Prepared Spells
entries: Whenever you gain a Jaeger level, you can replace one spell on your list with another Warlock spell for which you have spell slots.

entries.name: Spellcasting Ability
entries: Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for your Warlock spells.

entries.name: Spellcasting Focus
entries: You can use an {@item Arcane Focus|XPHB} as a {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB} for your Warlock spells.

subclassFeature.name: Shrouded Steps
entries: When you use a Jaeger feature that allows you to move without spending your movement (such as {@optfeature Jaeger's Rush|SGttEH24} or {@optfeature Chasing|SGttEH24} Finisher), you can teleport the distance moved instead. When you do so for {@optfeature Dodge Step|SGttEH24}, you automatically succeed on the saving throw to evade the attack.

subclassFeature.name: Mystical Momentum
entries: When you expend a spell slot, you gain a number of Momentum dice equal to the level of the spell slot expended.

subclassFeature.name: Darkness Within
entries: You can unleash the twisted powers you've bound to your soul as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. For 1 minute, you become wreathed in darkness. You gain the following effects:

items: The area in a 10-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} centered on you dims. {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} becomes {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB}, and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} becomes {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB}.
You are {@variantrule Heavily Obscured|XPHB} by swirling shadows.
You gain a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} of 30 feet.

You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage. Whenever you take Radiant damage you lose these {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB|Resistances} until the start of your next turn.

entries: These effects end early if you become {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB}, or end this feature early as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you complete a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Marauder Chapter
entries: {@i A jaeger of the Marauder Chapter embodies raw slaughter. Vengeance, wrath, and death, all rolled into one swing that never ceases, endlessly cleaving through monsters, leaving a bloody wake of destruction. Each marauder is a symbol of the Chapter's determination and savagery, striking fear into the hearts of their enemies with their every swing. They are unstoppable warriors, driven by a thirst for vengeance and the need to protect humanity from the monstrous hordes.}

subclassFeature.name: Marauder Momentum
entries: While you are wielding a melee weapon with the {@itemProperty 2H|XPHB|Two-Handed} property, the size of your Momentum die is increased by one step (from a {@dice d6} to a {@dice d8}, to a {@dice d10} at 11th level, and to a {@dice d12} at 17th level).

subclassFeature.name: Path of Gore
entries: You learn the {@optfeature Great Cleave|SGttEH24} Finisher. This does not count against your Finishers known.

subclassFeature.name: Unbreakable
entries: You wade through your enemies relying on your toughness and grit. Your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} Maximum increases by 3, and it increases by 1 whenever you gain another Jaeger level.
While you are wearing Medium Armor, you can add your Constitution modifier (to a maximum of +2) instead of your Dexterity modifier to determine your {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Leap Attack
entries: Once per turn, if you move more than 15 feet toward a creature (or fall 10 feet or more) immediately before making a weapon attack against them, you deal extra damage on a hit equal to your Momentum die.

When falling, you can make this attack prior to hitting the ground if there is a target creature in range where you would fall. On a hit, any damage you would take as a result of falling is reduced by half, and you do not fall {@condition Prone|XPHB} from the fall.

subclassFeature.name: Fell the Leviathan
entries: Whenever you use a Finisher, the target must succeed on a Strength saving throw (DC = 8 + your Strength modifier + your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}) or be knocked {@condition Prone|XPHB}. Creatures that are Large or larger have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on this saving throw.

subclassFeature.name: Titanic Strength
entries: Your strength is so great that you can wield a {@itemProperty 2H|XPHB|Two-Handed} weapon in one hand, and can use {@action Two-Weapon Fighting|XPHB} even when the weapons you are wielding aren't {@itemProperty L|XPHB|Light} (including using {@itemProperty 2H|XPHB|Two-Handed} weapons).
If you use two hands to wield a {@itemProperty 2H|XPHB|Two-Handed} weapon, your attacks with it deal extra damage equal to half your Strength modifier (rounded up) on a hit.

Additionally, when you use a Finisher, you can double the number of Momentum dice you have (to no more than your maximum Momentum dice + 1). Once you've doubled your dice, you can't do so again until you complete a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Salvation Chapter
entries: {@i A divine force endows jaegers of the Salvation Chapter with the ability to defend the world from the horrors, purging evil with one hand while saving the innocent with the other. Favorites of the Radiant Church, they are the light of hope in the darkness where evil dwells. Members of the Salvation Chapter know that prevention is always better than cure, and that murder is the most effective method of prevention. In their zealous righteousness, they offer hope of sanity and sanctuary to those that stand with them in their war against the tides of madness.}

subclassFeature.name: Art of Salvation
entries: You learn the {@optfeature Prayer of Salvation|SGttEH24} Adrenaline Art. This does not count against your Adrenaline Arts known.

subclassFeature.name: Savior's Focus
entries: You find new strength in the most desperate hours. When an allied creature you can see or hear is reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, you regain 1 Adrenaline Point. Once you use this feature, you can't do so again for 1 minute.

subclassFeature.name: Sanctifying Light
entries: When you expend an Adrenaline Point, you can choose to emit a glow of divine light until the end of your next turn. The area in a 20-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} centered on you is {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB}, and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} an additional 20 feet beyond that. The light is Sunlight. Any allied creature that enters the {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there gains {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to {@dice 1d6} + your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}. This light is extinguished early if you become {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Purifying Salvation
entries: Your {@optfeature Prayer of Salvation|SGttEH24} Adrenaline Art can target an additional creature, and the number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} it restores and {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} it grants increases to {@dice 1d12}. Additionally, if any of the targets have the {@condition Charmed|XPHB}, {@condition Frightened|XPHB} or {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition, or suffer from a Short-Term Madness, you can cleanse the condition from them, ending it.

subclassFeature.name: Light of Hope
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can unleash a blinding light from within for 1 minute, illuminating the darkness. You gain the following benefits for the duration:

items: The {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} of your Sanctifying Light increases to 30 feet of {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} and 30 feet of {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} beyond it. Each creature of your choice (including yourself) within the {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} are under the effect of {@spell Bless|XPHB}. Creatures in the {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule Death Saving Throw|XPHB|Death Saving Throws}, do not die at three failed saves, and become stable with three successful saves, even if they have three or more failed saves. A creature that is no longer within the {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} of this effect and is not stable dies if they have failed three or more death saving throws.
When you use {@optfeature Prayer of Salvation|SGttEH24}, you immediately regain the expended Adrenaline Point.

entries: These benefits end early if you become {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB}, or end this feature early as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Sanguine Chapter
entries: {@i In order to strengthen themselves, jaegers of the Sanguine Chapter rely on the mastery of blood. They achieve resilient states of unnatural life and feed their powers with the fruits of their violence. Although some consider them to be monsters, when faced with the horrors of the Scourge, the sentiment quickly changes. These jaeger's abilities can hold the line and preserve the tenuous flame of life when it should have long since been extinguished, keeping themselves and their allies alive while draining the strength of their enemies in an endless crimson parade of blood.}

subclassFeature.name: Crimson Rage
entries: When you become {@status Bloodied|XPHB}, you regain 1 Adrenaline Point and gain 1 Momentum die. You cannot benefit from this feature again for 1 minute.

subclassFeature.name: Vital Consumption
entries: You gain the ability to draw power from the flowing blood of your foes. You learn the {@optfeature Blood Drain|SGttEH24} Finisher, which does not count against your Finishers known. Some of your abilities use a Blood Magic save DC, which is calculated as follows:

subclassFeature.name: Blood Hex
entries: When you use a Finisher against a target with {@status Bloodied|XPHB} condition, you can force one target hit by the Finisher to make a Constitution saving throw against your Blood Magic save DC, selecting one of the following effects. You can use one of these effects when using your {@optfeature Blood Drain|SGttEH24} Finisher even if the target does not have the {@status Bloodied|XPHB} condition, and when using that Finisher it uses the Finisher's saving throw rather than making a separate saving throw.

items.name: Blood Puppet
items.entry: The target must use its {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to move up to 5 feet in a direction of your choice (if the target's {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} is unavailable, it cannot move).

items.name: Bound Blood
items.entry: The target's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is halved until the start of your next turn.

items.name: Burning Blood
items.entry: The target takes Fire damage equal to half the Necrotic damage dealt.

subclassFeature.name: Empowered Blood Hex
entries: You can invoke more powerful blood hexes. You gain the following options for your Blood Hex feature, each of which are improved versions of the hexes granted by Blood Hex, but cost 2 Adrenaline Points to invoke.

items.name: Blood Puppet (Empowered)
items.entry: The target must use its {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to move up to 10 feet and make a single weapon attack against a creature of your choice (if the target's {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} is unavailable, it cannot move).

items.name: Bound Blood (Empowered)
items.entry: The target gains the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition until the start of your next turn.

items.name: Burning Blood (Empowered)
items.entry: At the start of each of its turns, the target takes Fire damage equal to the Necrotic damage dealt. The target can repeat the saving throw it made against the {@optfeature Blood Drain|SGttEH24} Finisher at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success.

subclassFeature.name: Blood Frenzy
entries: While you have the {@status Bloodied|XPHB} condition you gain the following benefits:

items: Your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is increased by 20 feet.
When you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action, the number of attacks you make increases to three.

All {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} you regain from your Jaeger abilities are doubled. If an ability would restore you to no longer having the {@status Bloodied|XPHB} condition, the effects of this ability do not fade until the end of your turn.
You can use the {@optfeature Blood Craze|SGttEH24} Adrenaline Art.
